beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, walk the dog around the block.
beats.reasoning: Monday morning wake-up at home in Roosevelt with full energy. Asa starts the day with routine and dog companionship, setting a grounded tone for the week.

beats.activity: Commute to the SoDo optometry practice.
beats.reasoning: Drive south from Roosevelt to SoDo; Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District doesn't affect this route. Budget 35 min for across-town drive on a sunny Monday morning.

beats.activity: Morning and early afternoon optometry appointments, patient consultations, and frame fitting.
beats.reasoning: Asa's workday begins with full patient schedule. The 11am FIFA match (USA vs Wales) is happening, but Asa is direct and work-focused—the match doesn't interrupt the practice. Scheduled through lunch hour to maintain clinic flow.

beats.activity: Lunch break; grab a quick sandwich and coffee nearby.
beats.reasoning: Asa takes a pragmatic lunch break in SoDo. Sunny weather and generous spending habits mean a quality meal; nearby Uwajimaya offers good options without leaving the neighborhood.

beats.activity: Afternoon optometry appointments, eye exams, and administrative tasks.
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon clinic session. Asa's practice-based routine continues; the sunny weather doesn't pull them away from patient care responsibilities.
Drive north from SoDo back to Roosevelt after work. Budget 35 min for the return trip on a Monday evening with light traffic.

beats.activity: Walk the dog, decompress from the day.
beats.reasoning: Back at home; Asa spends quality time with the dog after work. The sunny, 74F evening is perfect for a short walk and unwinding before dinner.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Asa prefers close friends and quiet time over crowds; a home-cooked dinner on a beautiful Monday evening fits their personality and energy.

beats.activity: Browse vinyl collection, listen to records, and work on pottery project.
beats.reasoning: Asa unwinds with two of their favorite hobbies—vinyl collecting and pottery—on a relaxing Monday evening. This aligns with their personality and low-key energy after a full workday.

beats.activity: Wind down: light reading or scrolling, prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Final wind-down at home before sleep. Asa is already settled in for the night, creating a natural transition to bedtime.
Full 8-hour sleep cycle at home, ending at 6:30am the next morning. Asa had a productive Monday and is well-rested for Tuesday.

diary:
Monday morning felt easy—dog and I caught the sunrise on our block, and the weather's been perfect. Work was steady; a mix of routine exams and a tricky frame fitting that actually went well. I grabbed a sandwich at Uwajimaya during lunch and noticed the bars downtown were already buzzing about the World Cup match. Somehow I managed to miss all that chaos, which felt like a small win given the Light Rail was down anyway.

Came home early enough to decompress properly. Made dinner, then spent a couple hours with my vinyl collection—threw on some old jazz records and worked on a bowl I've been shaping for weeks. There's something grounding about that. By evening I was ready to just wind down with a book. Feeling settled, honestly. The kind of day that doesn't ask much of you.
thought_process: I was aware the World Cup was happening, but honestly it didn't pull at me—I'm not really caught up in that energy this year. The Light Rail closure could've been annoying, but my commute is straightforward enough that it didn't matter. I think I was just grateful for a normal Monday: patients who were easy to help, no drama, nothing unexpected. The real shift came after work when I gave myself permission to just be quiet. I chose vinyl and pottery over scrolling because I needed to do something with my hands, something tactile. The day felt like it had good rhythm—push during work hours, then genuine release at home. That balance made all the difference in my mood.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
